text: Struggle to apply AI. Through its investors, the laboratory has been able to build enormous development capacity, both in terms of computing power and scarce AI skills. This stands in stark contrast to the struggle of the average company to embed such technology in their daily practice. In 2020, only 13 per cent of Dutch companies used one or more AI technologies, according to figures from [CBS](https://opendata.cbs.nl/#/CBS/nl/dataset/84830NED/barh?ts=1670493464021).

metadata.description: ChatGPT can handle the most diverse requests. Meanwhile, companies struggle to put AI into practice. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: Struggle to apply AI. Through its investors, the laboratory has been able to build enormous development capacity, both in terms of computing power and scarce AI skills. This stands in stark contrast to the struggle of the average company to embed such technology in their daily practice. In 2020, only 13 per cent of Dutch companies used one or more AI technologies, according to figures from [CBS](https://opendata.cbs.nl/#/CBS/nl/dataset/84830NED/barh?ts=1670493464021).
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide
metadata.tags: Column, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Digitalisering, AI

id: Advertentiemodel onder druk door nieuwe Europese wetgeving__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0075
text: Although it is not yet possible to determine exactly how many companies in the Netherlands will be affected, it is already clear that many websites depend on advertising revenue. Of the ten million most-visited websites worldwide, almost [47 per cent](https://w3techs.com/technologies/history_overview/advertising/all "Historical trends in the usage statistics of advertising networks for websites") use an ‘ad network’. This is a technological solution that aggregates online ad space from publishers and offers it to advertisers. The extent to which these websites will still be able to generate revenue through personalised advertising is likely to decline sharply. The new process requirements make it easier for internet users to refuse tracking. The consequences of redesigning such a process should not be underestimated.

metadata.source_slug: Advertentiemodel onder druk door nieuwe Europese wetgeving
metadata.description: This article explains how the Digital Services Act will make ‘tracking’ individuals for advertising purposes more difficult and lists alternatives.
metadata.tags: Analyse, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Retail, Leisure, Digitalisering, Wet- en regelgeving
